portfolio.name: Lee Seoung Hyun
portfolio.intro: I am a developer who constantly thinks about efficiency. I automate repetitive tasks and simplify complex structures. Unable to end the day without solving problems, my mind continues to asynchronously compute solutions even after work.

meta.title: Seoung Hyun Lee Portfolio
meta.description: Portfolio of Seoung Hyun Lee, a frontend developer who pursues efficiency.

inu.title: Graduated from Incheon National University
inu.description:
Studied at Incheon National University with a double major in English Literature and Computer Science.
Overall GPA: 3.7
Computer Science Major GPA: 4.02

codeit.title: Codeit Sprint FE Bootcamp
codeit.description:
Intensively studied frontend development for 6 months at Codeit Sprint FE Bootcamp.
Developed collaboration experience and problem-solving skills through real projects using various tools and technologies.

data.growthJourney:
- During high school, I focused on learning while contemplating my career path, cultivating consistency and self-direction.
- In university, I experienced the joy of communication and knowledge sharing through presentation activities.
- At a VR theme park, I observed how planning and programs impact user experience in the field, sparking my interest in user-centered development and starting my frontend development journey.
- Through a bootcamp, I learned and applied frontend technologies while developing problem-solving skills.
- In professional work, I'm growing both my technical and communication capabilities.

details: Reduced test execution time by 67.7% relative to test count through parallel test processing using Dynamic metrics and Playwright Sharding
Fully migrated existing ESLint + Prettier-based lint automation to Biome, reducing average CI time by 98% and improving overall development speed
Developed custom onboarding guide component and improved personalized copy, achieving 42% increase in new user payment calendar entry rate and 17% increase in mobile revisit rate
Improved search efficiency through structured metadata addition and streamlined management through internal documentation
Identified missing metadata issues per page and abstracted into common logic to streamline management
Improved responsive image component with higher quality images, reducing CLS from 0.99 to 0.1 and LCP from 1.62 to 0.55
Actively used the performance tab to precisely identify areas requiring memoization and resolved unnecessary flickering
Documented issues with the existing server action-based communication architecture and established team-wide API architecture standards

features.purpose: Security Design and Certification Process for ISMS
details: Designed and implemented authorization logic based on user permissions
Designed and implemented token handling logic
Joined mid-migration from PHP to Next.js monorepo and minimized user confusion through component design that preserved existing UI/UX
Introduced MSW to improve mock data management efficiency and simplify asynchronous communication handling
Migrated CRA-based project to Vite, eliminating security risks
Replaced editor with react-quill-new and custom module react-quill-resize to address internal CSP policy and ensure image upload stability
Reduced maximum bundle size from 1200kB to 500kB (58.3% reduction) through Rollup optimization
Improved structural stability and predictability by separating server state and UI state that were previously coupled in zustand
Enhanced maintainability efficiency through feature-based folder structure transition and documentation
Implemented and optimized custom PDF Viewer using Canvas API
Reduced initial rendering time by 67% (5401ms → 1780ms) through Web Worker, react-window, and code splitting
Migrated CRA-based project to Vite, resolving CSP and performance issues
Reduced Docker image size from 2.22GB to 195MB (91.36% reduction) by applying multi-stage build and Standalone mode
Reduced bundle size from 302kB to 143kB (73.5% reduction) by removing unnecessary modules and using dynamic import

designSystem.title: SamilDevKit Design System
designSystem.description: Building a design system to strengthen internal design consistency and development productivity

details: Led the adoption of clsx and cva to ensure conditional styling and class naming consistency
Improved development quality and ensured team code consistency through ESLint, TypeScript configuration refinement and documentation

reindeer.title: Reindeer's Letter
reindeer.description: A service for writing and sending scheduled letters in voice and text format to yourself and others at year-end

details: Systematized request cancellation and error management through a common network request hook using AbortController, improving network stability and user experience
Resolved in-app browser limitations by implementing Chrome browser switching functionality before script loading
Applied List Virtualization using react-window, optimizing performance and reducing rendering time by approximately 80%

kkomKkom.title: KkomKkom
kkomKkom.description: A service for forming teams and managing schedules to become meticulous, bootcamp final project

details: Developed overlay system through zustand-based useOverlay custom hook, referencing toss useOverlay
Implemented fetch function for refresh token rotation using server actions
Implemented smooth state transition UX through progress bar implementation using startTransition

aboutMe.title: About Me
paragraphs: [B]I am a developer who constantly thinks about efficiency.[/B] I automate repetitive tasks and simplify complex structures. Unable to end the day without solving problems, my mind continues to [B]asynchronously compute solutions[/B] even after work.

[B]I believe documentation is a key tool that boosts both my productivity and my team's.[/B] I always record what I learn in personal notes and share them as blog posts when helpful. I'm confident these documents serve as useful guides not only for me, but also for fellow developers facing similar challenges.

allra.summary: Improved developer productivity through [B]test and CI pipeline enhancements[/B], and achieved [B]LCP reduced by 66%, CLS improved by up to 90%[/B] via image optimization and re-render structure improvements. Contributed to a [B]42% increase in settlement calendar entry rate through SEO structure improvements[/B], alongside [B]open source contributions[/B] and browser security vulnerability analysis.

items.title: "Digital Finance" Service Management Portal Development
items.detail: Secured [B]58.3% bundle size reduction[/B] and stable state management via CRA-to-Vite migration and Tanstack Query adoption. Resolved security issues by developing a custom editor module for [B]CSP policy compliance[/B].

items.title: "Document AI" AI-powered Report Analysis Solution Development
items.detail: Achieved [B]67% initial render time reduction[/B] via custom PDF Viewer with pdf.js and Canvas API, and [B]91.36% image size, 73.5% bundle reduction[/B] via Docker multi-stage builds and dynamic import.

items.title: "SamilDevKit" Design System Development
items.detail: Optimized rendering through CSS/JavaScript role separation and GPU acceleration, and secured styling consistency via [B]clsx-cva adoption[/B].

allra.title: Allra
allra.subtitle: Allra Fintech
allra.description: Pre-settlement financial service development for e-commerce sellers

details.text: Configured dynamic parallel test environment using [B]Dynamic metric[/B] and [B]Playwright Sharding[/B], reducing average test time by [B]67.7%[/B]
Replaced [B]ESLint + Prettier[/B] lint pipeline with [B]Biome[/B], reducing average CI lint time by [B]98%[/B]

Led [B]Datadog → OpenReplay[/B] cost-saving migration. Debugged Next.js group route sourcemap upload errors, configured CI/CD pipeline, and discovered missing handleError during source analysis, contributing via [B]open source PR[/B]
Developed custom onboarding guide with personalized messaging by user state, achieving [B]42% improvement[/B] in settlement calendar entry rate and [B]17% improvement[/B] in mobile return visits
Identified missing page metadata, abstracted into shared logic with structured metadata, improving search efficiency and reducing maintenance cost. Documented guidelines to boost team efficiency
Analyzed Chrome CPP source to identify autocomplete ignore cause, resolved with [B]Honeypot decoy field approach to bypass autofill[/B]
Refactored pre-settlement request logic with [B]TypeScript Discriminated Union[/B], enforcing impossible states at type level to prevent runtime errors and improve readability
Refactored bidirectional pre-settlement lookup logic to unidirectional data flow using Context API
Minimized code change scope when adding features intertwined with pre-settlement lookup logic such as early-seller programs
Improved build speed and developer experience by [B]migrating to Vite[/B] from CRA
Enhanced bundling efficiency with [B]Rollup optimization[/B]
Achieved bundle size [B]1200kB → 500kB (58.3% reduction)[/B]
Implemented [B]network call optimization and state responsibility separation[/B] via Tanstack Query adoption
Simplified components and improved [B]UX and predictability[/B] by [B]clearly separating server and UI state[/B]
Improved cohesion by [B]transitioning to Folder-by-Feature structure[/B]
[B]Designed a hybrid structure mixing role-based and feature-based approaches[/B]
Improved [B]code consistency and maintenance efficiency through documentation[/B]
Built custom [B]PDF Viewer[/B] based on [B]pdf.js and Canvas API[/B]
Async rendering via [B]Web Worker[/B] and virtualization of large documents via [B]react-window[/B] → [B]67% initial render improvement[/B]
[B]Docker image optimization[/B]
subItems: Achieved [B]2.22GB → 195MB (91.36% reduction)[/B] via [B]standalone mode and build optimization[/B]

details.text: Bundle [B]302kB → 143kB (73.5% reduction)[/B] via dynamic import
[B]Led the adoption of clsx and cva[/B] to secure styling consistency and improve maintenance efficiency
